duration: Ten minutes then 20 more minutes an hour later
description: Brenda Wright first found the bird feeding with other shorebirds. Adult, plumage was in a transitional state so likely a first year Ruddy Turnstone that was out of range. Medium size shorebird about the size of a Killdeer. Red orange legs, white underparts, dark brown bill, rufous brown back, dark area extending down chest and dark areas on the face on a whitish background. We were careful not to spook the bird so did not see the bird in flight.
